essence: Designs, improves, and optimises CNC machining processes to increase efficiency, accuracy, and production quality.

identity.title: CNC Process Engineer
identity.summary: Develops machining processes, optimises toolpaths, improves cycle times, and ensures CNC production meets engineering, quality, and efficiency standards.

salary.notes: Process engineers in aerospace, defence, or high‑precision manufacturing can exceed £90k-£120k+.

qualifications: Experience in CNC machining, programming, or applications engineering
Strong understanding of tooling, materials, feeds, speeds, and machining strategies
Ability to analyse and optimise machining processes
Knowledge of lean manufacturing, quality control, and engineering principles

lifestyle.description: CNC process engineers work in advanced manufacturing, aerospace, automotive, and precision engineering. They design machining processes, improve efficiency, reduce waste, and ensure consistent production quality. The role suits individuals who enjoy engineering analysis, optimisation, and improving manufacturing performance.

habits: Develops and optimises CNC machining processes
Analyses cycle times, tooling performance, and workflow
Collaborates with machinists, programmers, and engineers
Implements lean manufacturing and continuous improvement
